seoTitle: Denmark vs Devils Creek — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Denmark and Devils Creek. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Denmark vs Devils Creek: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Denmark and Devils Creek.
heading: Denmark vs Devils Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Denmark, Western Australia and Devils Creek, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Devils Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Denmark (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Denmark or Devils Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Denmark has a flood risk score of 48/100, while Devils Creek scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Denmark scores 25/100 for bushfire risk. Devils Creek scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Denmark or Devils Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Devils Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
